assertion_results.text: JQL queries use the project key (TC) and vulnerability issue type ID (10024) from the Security Configuration in claude-md-security-config.md — not hardcoded values (§1.49)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 0 in discovery-listing.md explicitly extracts 'Project key: TC' and 'Vulnerability issue type ID: 10024' from the Security Configuration and states 'These values are used to construct the JQL queries below.' All three JQL queries use 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024', confirming the values come from configuration, not hardcoded.

assertion_results.text: Three separate queries are constructed: one for untriaged issues (labels NOT IN ai-cve-triaged), one for triaged-but-new issues (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status = New), and one for Ready for QA candidates (labels IN ai-cve-triaged AND status NOT IN Closed, Verified, ON_QA) (§1.48)
assertion_results.evidence: Three JQL queries are explicitly shown in discovery-listing.md: Query 1: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels NOT IN (ai-cve-triaged)'; Query 2: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status = New'; Query 3: 'project = TC AND issuetype = 10024 AND labels IN (ai-cve-triaged) AND status NOT IN (Closed, Verified, 'ON_QA')'. All three match the expected patterns.

assertion_results.text: Results are grouped into three clearly separated lists: Untriaged issues, Triaged but still New issues, and Ready for QA issues
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-listing.md contains three clearly separated sections with headers: 'Query 1: Untriaged Issues', 'Query 2: Triaged but still New', and 'Query 3: Ready for QA', each separated by horizontal rules (---).

assertion_results.text: Each listed issue shows: issue key, status, CVE ID (from labels), summary, and created date
assertion_results.evidence: Every listed issue includes all five fields. For example, TC-9001 shows: issue key (TC-9001), status (New), CVE ID (CVE-2026-40112), summary ('h2 - HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability [rhtpa-2.2]'), and created date (2026-06-08). The same pattern applies to all issues across all three queries.

assertion_results.text: Status-aware handling: New issues (TC-9001, TC-9002, TC-9004) are presented for full triage, while the In Progress issue (TC-9003) triggers a warning about active work
assertion_results.evidence: In status-handling.md: TC-9001, TC-9002, and TC-9004 all have 'Handling: Proceed with full triage (default path)' and 'Action: Present for full triage.' TC-9003 has 'Handling: Warning — active work detected' with the message 'This issue is already in In Progress. It may be actively worked on.' and presents options to proceed or skip.

assertion_results.text: Ready for QA filtering: TC-9020 appears in Ready for QA (all linked Tasks Done/Closed), TC-9023 is excluded (TC-9025 still In Progress), TC-9026 is excluded (no Depend links)
assertion_results.evidence: In discovery-listing.md: TC-9020 is in the 'Ready for QA — Qualified Issues' table with remediation tasks 'TC-9021 (Done), TC-9022 (Closed)'. TC-9023 is in 'Excluded from Ready for QA' with reason 'TC-9025 is still In Progress'. TC-9026 is excluded with reason 'No linked Tasks with type Depend — no remediation to verify'. The Filtering Logic section confirms all three decisions.
